diagrams.title: Rule of Sum Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows two mutually exclusive sets of choices with no overlap
elements: Set A with m elements (e.g., 3 shirts)
Set B with n elements (e.g., 4 pants)
diagrams.purpose: Understand when to add - when choosing one OR another (mutually exclusive)

diagrams.title: Rule of Product Tree Diagram
diagrams.description: Tree showing sequential independent choices branching at each step
elements: Each branch splits into (n-1) branches for second choice
Continue for r steps showing total n(n-1)(n-2)... paths
diagrams.purpose: Visualize multiplication principle - each path represents one complete selection sequence

diagrams.title: Permutation Slot Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows r slots to be filled from n available items where order matters
diagrams.purpose: Understand how permutations count ordered arrangements

diagrams.title: Combination Selection Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows unordered selection from n items
elements: Selection circle showing r items chosen
Brute force: Choose any r from n
diagrams.purpose: Understand combinations as selections without regard to order

diagrams.title: Pigeonhole Principle Illustration
diagrams.description: Visual demonstration of distributing items into containers
elements: Distribution showing at least one box has ceil(10/4)=3 items
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the basic pigeonhole principle and its application
